name: NCCL No Credit Check Loans
description_short: NCCL No Credit Check Loans Waukesha, Wisconsin — Texas-based loan broker with 63 storefronts offering payday loans, installment loans, and check cashing...
description_long:
NCCL No Credit Check Loans launched in 2020 with a stated mission of expanding credit access to borrowers who are typically turned away by traditional lenders. The company grew quickly across Texas, building a physical storefront network and, in 2022, became part of the Magnolia Payday Loans company. As of their website, NCCL operates 63 store locations, with branches in Fort Worth, Dallas, Houston, San Antonio, Amarillo, Lubbock, Waco, and Austin. In 2023 they announced a partnership with BadCredify, a financial literacy website.

NCCL's core products are payday loans and installment loans for amounts up to $1,000. They also offer money orders and check cashing services at their physical locations, making them a multi-service financial services storefront rather than a pure lender. Borrowers can apply in-store or online. Eligibility requirements are minimal: applicants must be at least 18, a U.S. resident, hold a valid ID, demonstrate steady income, and have an active bank account and contact information.

A critical distinction is that NCCL is explicitly not a direct lender. Their About Us page states they work with 'a huge base of trusted lending companies' to match borrowers with loan offers. This means actual loan terms — including APR, fees, and repayment schedules — are set by third-party partner lenders, not NCCL itself. The company positions this as an advantage, claiming access to a wider range of lending options for any credit profile. Store hours are extended compared to many competitors, running weekdays 10am–6pm and weekends 10am–4pm.

For borrowers in genuine emergencies with damaged credit, NCCL provides a real option when banks and credit unions have said no. However, payday and installment loans from broker networks consistently carry very high APRs, and NCCL does not disclose rate ranges on their public-facing pages. The brokerage model also means the borrower cannot know the exact lender or terms until they apply. Geographic coverage is limited to Texas. Consumers should treat this as a last-resort borrowing option and confirm all rate and fee details before signing.
pros: No credit check — explicitly accepts bad credit applicants
Up to $1,000 available quickly with minimal eligibility requirements
63 physical store locations across 8 Texas cities for in-person service
Extended weekend hours (10am–4pm Saturday and Sunday)
Multiple financial services available in one location: loans, money orders, and check cashing
Both online and in-store application options
Partnership with BadCredify provides supplemental financial literacy resources
cons: Not a direct lender — loan terms, APR, and fees are set by undisclosed third-party partners and vary by match
No rate or APR information published anywhere on the public website
Service is limited exclusively to Texas; no coverage in other states
Loan maximum of $1,000 is low and may not cover larger emergencies
Payday loan products carry inherently high costs and short repayment windows typical of this loan category
best_for: Texas residents with bad or no credit who need up to $1,000 quickly
Borrowers who prefer or require in-person financial service
People who need multiple services (loan + money order or check cashing) in one visit
Applicants who have been declined elsewhere due to credit history
diagnosis: NCCL is a viable last-resort option for Texas borrowers with bad credit who need up to $1,000 fast and either prefer in-person service or live near one of their 8 Texas locations. The main caveat is that NCCL is a broker, not a direct lender — actual loan costs are determined by partner lenders and are not disclosed upfront, so borrowers must review all terms carefully before committing.
services: Loan brokerage (matching to partner lender network)
